---
title: "Exploratory_Data_Analysis"
author: "Helen Wei Jia Chen"
output:
  html_document: default
  pdf_document: default
---

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# INSTALL REQUIRED PACKAGES
# Uncomment the lines below if you haven't installed these packages yet:
# install.packages("tidyverse")
# install.packages("readr")
# install.packages("psych")
# install.packages("moments")

library(tidyverse)   # includes dplyr, tidyr, ggplot2
library(readr)
library(psych)
library(moments)
```

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# LOAD DATASETS
data_wilcox <- read_csv("analysis_MTSE_FLTSE_calcs.csv")
data_alpha  <- read.csv("analysis_MTSE_FLTSE_cronbachs_alpha.csv")
data_alpha  <- data_alpha[1:145, ]
```

```{r}
# WILCOXON RANKED TEST: CHECKING ASSUMPTIONS

# Inspect structure
str(data_wilcox)

# Check for missing values
sum(is.na(data_wilcox$MTSE))
sum(is.na(data_wilcox$FLTSE))

# Remove rows with missing values and check numeric types
paired_data <- na.omit(data_wilcox[, c("MTSE", "FLTSE")])
is.numeric(paired_data$MTSE)
is.numeric(paired_data$FLTSE)

# Compute difference scores and summary
diff_scores <- paired_data$MTSE - paired_data$FLTSE
summary(diff_scores)

# Histogram of difference scores
ggplot(data.frame(diff_scores), aes(x = diff_scores)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.5, fill = "steelblue", color = "black") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Histogram of Difference Scores (MTSE - FLTSE)")

# Boxplot of difference scores
ggplot(data.frame(diff_scores), aes(y = diff_scores)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "orange") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Boxplot of Difference Scores")

# Skewness (should be close to 0 for symmetry)
skewness(diff_scores)

# Number of zero differences
sum(diff_scores == 0)
```

```{r}
# WILCOXON SIGNED RANK TEST: RUN AND REPORT

N    <- nrow(paired_data)
test <- wilcox.test(paired_data$MTSE, paired_data$FLTSE, paired = TRUE, exact = FALSE)

# Z approximation and effect size r
z <- qnorm(test$p.value / 2) * -sign(median(paired_data$MTSE - paired_data$FLTSE))
r <- z / sqrt(N)

cat("Wilcoxon Signed-Rank Test Results\n")
cat("-------------------------------\n")
cat("N =", N, "\n")
cat("V =", test$statistic, "\n")
cat("p =", test$p.value, "\n")
cat("Median MTSE =", median(paired_data$MTSE), "\n")
cat("Median FLTSE =", median(paired_data$FLTSE), "\n")
cat("Z =", z, "\n")
cat("Effect size r =", r, "\n")
```

```{r}
# CRONBACH'S ALPHA + APA REPORT

summarize_alpha_apa <- function(items, scale_name) {
  items <- data.frame(lapply(items, function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x))))
  items <- items[rowSums(is.na(items)) < ncol(items), ]

  alpha_res <- alpha(items)

  item_total_cor <- sapply(1:ncol(items), function(i) {
    cor(items[, i], rowSums(items[, -i, drop = FALSE], na.rm = TRUE),
        use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
  })

  alpha_value    <- round(alpha_res$total$raw_alpha, 2)
  min_itc        <- round(min(item_total_cor,  na.rm = TRUE), 2)
  mean_itc       <- round(mean(item_total_cor, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
  cor_matrix     <- cor(items, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
  inter_cor_mean <- round(mean(cor_matrix[lower.tri(cor_matrix)], na.rm = TRUE), 2)

  # Interpret alpha
  interpretation <- dplyr::case_when(
    alpha_value >= 0.90 ~ "Excellent",
    alpha_value >= 0.80 ~ "Good",
    alpha_value >= 0.70 ~ "Acceptable",
    alpha_value >= 0.60 ~ "Questionable",
    TRUE                ~ "Poor"
  )

  cat("--------------------------------------------------\n")
  cat(sprintf("Scale: %s (first 145 rows)\n", scale_name))
  cat(sprintf("Cronbach's alpha: %.2f (%s internal consistency)\n", alpha_value, interpretation))
  cat(sprintf("Mean item-total correlation: %.2f (minimum = %.2f)\n", mean_itc, min_itc))
  cat(sprintf("Mean inter-item correlation: %.2f\n\n", inter_cor_mean))
  cat(sprintf(
    "APA: The %s scale demonstrated %s internal consistency, α = %.2f, with mean item-total correlations of %.2f (minimum = %.2f) and a mean inter-item correlation of %.2f.\n\n",
    scale_name, tolower(interpretation), alpha_value, mean_itc, min_itc, inter_cor_mean
  ))
}

mtse_items  <- data_alpha[, c("MQ1", "MQ2", "MQ3", "MQ4")]
fltse_items <- data_alpha[, c("FQ1", "FQ2", "FQ3", "FQ4")]

summarize_alpha_apa(mtse_items,  "MTSE")
summarize_alpha_apa(fltse_items, "FLTSE")
```

```{r efa}
# EXPLORATORY FACTOR ANALYSIS

summarize_efa <- function(items, scale_name) {
  items <- data.frame(lapply(items, function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x))))
  items <- items[rowSums(is.na(items)) < ncol(items), ]

  efa_res        <- fa(items, nfactors = 1, fm = "ml", rotate = "none")
  loadings_values <- round(as.matrix(efa_res$loadings)[, 1], 2)

  cat("--------------------------------------------------\n")
  cat(sprintf("EFA Results: %s\n", scale_name))
  cat("Factor loadings:\n")
  print(loadings_values)
  cat(sprintf("\nRange of loadings: %.2f to %.2f\n\n",
              min(loadings_values), max(loadings_values)))
  cat(sprintf(
    "APA: Exploratory factor analysis supported unidimensionality for the %s scale, with factor loadings ranging from %.2f to %.2f.\n\n",
    scale_name, min(loadings_values), max(loadings_values)
  ))
}

summarize_efa(mtse_items,  "MTSE")
summarize_efa(fltse_items, "FLTSE")
```
